input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row.

indir.description: The directory where the input images are located (or where they will be cached once they are downloaded). If undefined, it defaults to <outdir>/input

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

data_options.title: Data Ingestion
data_options.description: Describe your input data that lives in the `input` directory

channels.description: List of channel names to process.
channels.help_text: Channel names are specified in the format "c[channel_number]", where the channel_number is 0-indexed.

resolution.description: Voxel resolution in all 3 dimensions. Default: 0.23,0.23,0.42
resolution.help_text: This is a comma-delimited tuple as x,y,z.

download_all_first.description: If set, wait for all input checks (downloads) to complete before starting stitching.

stitching_method.description: Stitching method to use: BigStitcher or SaalfeldStitcher

stitching_result_container.description: Name of the stitching result container

stitching_channel.description: Name of the channel used for stitching, e.g. 'c1' or '1'. You can also specify 'all' to use all of the channels. Default: 'all'

spark_cluster.description: Use a distributed cluster to run Spark jobs? Default: false
spark_cluster.help: By default, the Spark jobs will execute in a standalone Spark instance running as a single job. If you enable this option, a transient Spark cluster will be created on multiple nodes (controlled by the options below).

spark_workers.description: Number of Spark workers to use for stitching one acquisition. Default: 10

min_spark_workers.description: Minimum number of Spark workers needed to start stitching pipeline. Default: 1

spark_worker_cores.description: Number of cores allocated to each Spark worker. Default: 4

spark_worker_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory (in GB) allocated to each Spark worker. If not set, defaults to spark_worker_cores * spark_gb_per_core.

spark_executor_cores.description: Number of cores per Spark executor. If 0, defaults to spark_worker_cores.

spark_executor_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory (in GB) allocated to each Spark executor. If not set, defaults to spark_executor_cores * spark_gb_per_core.

spark_task_cores.description: Number of cores per Spark task. If 0, defaults to 1.

spark_driver_cores.description: Number of cores allocated for the Spark driver. Default: 1

spark_driver_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory to allocate for the Spark driver. Default: 14g

spark_gb_per_core.description: Size of memory (in GB) that is allocated for each core of a Spark worker. Default: 4
spark_gb_per_core.help_text: The total memory usage for stitching one acquisition will be workers * worker_cores * gb_per_core.

spark_local_dir.description: Temporary local folder for Spark processing. Must exist on all cluster nodes. Default: /tmp/spark-${workflow.sessionId}

spark_max_partition_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes to pack into a single partition when reading files. If empty, uses Spark default.

skip_bigstitcher_create_dataset.description: Skip dataset creation with BigStitcher (when the BigDataViewer XML is not used)

skip_bigstitcher_resave.description: Skip resave step with BigStitcher (when BigDataViewer XML is not used)

run_bigstitcher_detect_interestpoints.description: Run interest point detection with BigStitcher (SparkInterestPointDetection)

run_bigstitcher_match_interestpoints.description: Run interest point matching with BigStitcher (SparkGeometricDescriptorMatching)

advanced_stitching_params.description: BigStitcher stitching advanced args such as --ds --channels

flatfield_correction.description: Apply flatfield correction before stitching. Default: false

darkfieldfile.description: Path to the precomputed darkfield file used for flatfield correction

flatfieldfile.description: Path to the precomputed flatfield file used for flatfield correction

axis_mapping.description: Axis mapping for the objective->pixel coordinates conversion. Default: -x,y,z
axis_mapping.help_text: Comma-separated axis specification with optional flips.

stitching_block_size.description: Block size to use when converting input data to n5 before stitching. Default: 128,128,64

final_block_size_xy.description: Block size (in Z dimension) when retiling after stitching. Default: 64
final_block_size_xy.help_text: This must be smaller than the number of Z slices in the data.

stitching_padding.description: Padding for the overlap regions. Default: 0,0,0

stitching_blur_sigma.description: Sigma value of the gaussian blur pre-applied to the images before stitching. Default: 2

bigstream_config.description: Location of the YAML bigstream configuration

registration_compression.description: Compression codec used for registration output zarr containers

registration_compression_opts.description: Compression options (JSON) for registration output zarr containers

volume_expansion.description: Volume expansion applied to both fixed and moving volumes during registration and spot extraction

bigstream_max_concurrent_reads.description: Maximum number of concurrent zarr reads for bigstream

skip_registration.description: If true skip the entire registration, i.e., global alignment, local alignment, deformations and inverse

skip_local_align.description: If true skip local blockwise alignment

run_global_metric.description: If true, compute correlation metric after global registration using the same images and subpaths as global alignment

global_correlation_container.description: Output zarr container name for the global correlation metric

global_correlation_output_subpath.description: Subpath within the global correlation metric output container

run_local_metric.description: If true, compute correlation metric after local registration (deformations)

local_correlation_container.description: Output zarr container name for the local correlation metric

local_correlation_input_subpath.description: Subpath of the registered image with the deform field to use for the correlation metric

local_correlation_output_subpath.description: Subpath within the local correlation metric output container

neighborhood_for_metric_eval.description: Radius of the neighborhood used for correlation metric evaluation

skip_inverse.description: If true skip computing the inverse transformation

registration_fix_id.description: ID of the fix image used for registration

fix_global_subpath.description: Explicit fix image dataset used for global registration

fix_global_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for fix low resolution image

fix_global_channel.description: Explicit fix image channel used for global registration

mov_global_subpath.description: Explicit moving image dataset used for global registration

mov_global_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for moving low resolution image

mov_global_channel.description: Explicit fix image channel used for global registration

reuse_global_affine.description: If True and global affine exists, reuse it

local_fix_roi.description: Fix local mask block coordinates as zmin,ymin,xmin,zmax,ymax,xmax

local_mov_roi.description: Moving local mask block coordinates as zmin,ymin,xmin,zmax,ymax,xmax

global_steps.description: Global steps - if not set they are read from the bigstream config

fix_local_subpath.description: Fix dataset used for local registration

fix_local_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for fix high resolution image

fix_local_channel.description: Explicit channel for fix high resolution image

mov_local_subpath.description: Moving dataset used for local registration

mov_local_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for mov high resolution image

mov_local_channel.description: Explicit channel for mov high resolution image

global_mov_origin_transform.description: Initial transformation applied to the moving image origin for global registration

global_static_transforms.description: List of static transformations applied to global registration

fix_mask.description: Mask used for the fix image (applied to both global and local alignment). Absolute path, path relative to the registration output directory, or container filename when generate_fix_mask is true.

fix_mask_subpath.description: Dataset subpath within the fix mask container (used for both global and local alignment)

mov_mask.description: Mask used for the moving image (applied to both global and local alignment). Absolute path, path relative to the registration output directory, or container filename when generate_mov_mask is true.

mov_mask_subpath.description: Dataset subpath within the moving mask container (used for both global and local alignment)

local_mov_origin_transform.description: Initial transformation applied to the moving image origin for local registration

local_static_transforms.description: List of static transformations applied to local registration

generate_fix_mask.description: If true, generate the fix foreground mask using bigstream. The same mask is used for both global and local alignment. Uses fix_mask as the output container filename.

generate_mov_mask.description: If true, generate the moving foreground mask using bigstream. The same mask is used for both global and local alignment. Uses mov_mask as the output container filename.

foreground_mask_cpus.description: Number of CPUs for foreground mask generation

foreground_mask_mem_gb.description: Memory (GB) for foreground mask generation. 0 = use default_mem_gb_per_cpu * foreground_mask_cpus

mask_lambda.description: Controls the variance of the foreground region. A larger number means larger segment(s).

mask_smoothing.description: Number of iterations to apply mask smoothing - smaller value results in a rougher mask

mask_smooth_sigmas.description: One or more gaussian smoothing sigmas from the finest to the coarser resolution. Use larger sigmas for coarser resolutions.

mask_thresh_percentile.description: Intensity percentile used for finding the foreground threshold; when set, uses a simple threshold cutoff instead of the MorphACWE method

local_inverse_cpus.description: Number of cpus used for the main compute inverse process

local_inverse_mem_gb.description: Memory used by the main compute inverse process

local_deform_cpus.description: Number of cpus used for local deform driver

local_deform_mem_gb.description: Max memory in GB allowed for local deform driver

local_steps.description: Steps for local alignment - if not set they are read from bigstream config yaml

deform_subpath.description: subpath used for saving the deformation field

local_transform_name.description: Name of the container for the local deform transformation

local_inv_transform_name.description: Name of the container for the local inverse deform transformation

bigstream_deform_blocksize.description: Deformation zarr spatial chunk size as sizex,sizey,sizez

distributed_bigstream.description: If true use a distributed dask cluster for BigStream registration or deformation

warped_scales.description: All scales that need to be warped

global_align_cpus.description: Number of CPUs used for global registration

local_align_cpus.description: CPUs used for local alignment driver

local_align_mem_gb.description: Memory (GB) used for local alignment driver

bigstream_dask_workers.description: Number of Dask workers used for bigstream registration

bigstream_dask_min_workers.description: Minimum number of Dask workers that need to be available before the bigstream registration starts

bigstream_dask_worker_cpus.description: Number of CPUs for each bigstream Dask worker

bigstream_dask_worker_mem_gb.description: Memory (GB) for each bigstream Dask worker

bigstream_worker_retry.description: If true, retry bigstream Dask workers on certain exit statuses (130, 137)

bigstream_worker_max_retries.description: Maximum number of retries for bigstream Dask workers when bigstream_worker_retry is enabled

bigstream_local_blocksize.description: Processing size for computing the local alignment as sizex,sizey,sizez

bigstream_align_blocksize.description: Block size used for applying the alignment transform as sizex,sizey,sizez as well as deformed image chunksize

bigstream_local_overlap_factor.description: Block overlap used for local alignment

bigstream_local_fg_pct.description: Foreground percentage threshold used for local alignment

bigstream_local_deform_overlap.description: Block overlap factor used for applying the deformation transform

bigstream_local_inv_overlap_factor.description: Processing overlap factor used for computing the inverse transform

bigstream_inv_step.description: Step used for generating inverse displacement vector

bigstream_inv_iterations.description: Iterations for generating the inverse deformation field. This can be a comma separated list for using multiple iteration levels.

bigstream_inv_shrink_spacings.description: Comma separated list of shrink spacing values per level. The number of values here must equal the number of iteration levels defined by `bigstream_inv_iterations`.

bigstream_inv_smooth_sigmas.description: Comma separated list of smooth sigmas per level

bigstream_inv_step_cut_factor.description: Step cut factor used for generated inverse displacement vector

bigstream_inv_pad.description: Padding used for generated inverse displacement vector

bigstream_inv_use_root.description: Use root for generating the inverse displacement vector

bigstream_dashboard_port.description: Bigstream dask dashboard port - if 0 the scheduler will pick a port that is available. The typical port used by the dask dashboard is 8787

bigstream_scheduler_port.description: Bigstream dask scheduler port - if 0 the scheduler will pick a port that is available. The typical port for the dask scheduler is 8786

bigstream_dask_nthreads.description: Number of threads per Dask worker process

bigstream_dask_nprocs.description: Number of Dask worker processes per worker node. (Default to None)

global_registration_container.description: Name of the global registration container

global_registration_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for low resolution alignment output

global_transform_name.description: Name of the global affine transform output file

global_inv_transform_name.description: Name of the inverse global affine transform output file

local_registration_container.description: Name of the final registration container

local_registration_timeindex.description: selected timepoint for low resolution alignment output

global_results_subdir.description: Subdirectory used for global registration results

local_results_subdir.description: Subdirectory used for local registration results

distributed_spot_extraction.description: If true run spot extraction on a distributed spark cluster

skipped_spot_extraction_rounds.description: Comma-delimited list of round IDs for which spot extraction is skipped and default results are generated instead

extract_spots_from_warped.description: If true, extract spots from warped (registered) images instead of stitched images

spots_channels.description: Comma delimited list of spots channels. This together with spots_scale is used for determining the datasets used for spot extraction.

spots_excluded_channels.description: Comma delimited list of spot channels excluded from spot extraction. This together with spots_scale is used for determining the datasets used for.

spots_scale.description: image scale used for spot extraction. This together with spots_channels can be used for determining the datasets used for spot extraction if spots_subpath is not set.

spots_subpath.description: alternative for defining the datasets used for spot extraction. If this is defined it takes priority over spots_channels and spots_scale.

spots_image_name.description: NRRD filename for saving extracted spots as an image for inspection purposes.

spots_image_subpath_ref.description: Dataset subpath used to determine the shape of the output spots image. Typically a lower-resolution dataset to reduce the size of the inspection image.

rsfish_min_intensity.description: Minimal intensity of the image. Default: 0.

rsfish_max_intensity.description: Maximal intensity of the image. Default: 4096

rsfish_anisotropy.help_text: Scaling of z relative to xy. Can be determined using the RS-FISH anisotropy plugin in Fiji.

rsfish_sigma.description: Sigma value for Difference-of-Gaussian (DoG) calculation. Default 1.5

rsfish_threshold.description: Threshold value for Difference-of-Gaussian (DoG) calculation. Default: 0.007

rsfish_background.description: Background subtraction method, 0 == None, 1 == Mean, 2==Median, 3==RANSAC on Mean, 4==RANSAC on Median. Default: 0 (None)

rsfish_intensity_method.description: Intensity calculation method, 0 == Linear Interpolation, 1 == Gaussian fit (on inlier pixels), 2 == Integrate spot intensities (on candidate pixels). Default: 0 (Linear Interpolation)

rsfish_spark_workers.description: Number of Spark workers to use for spot extraction.

rsfish_min_spark_workers.description: Minimum number of Spark workers needed to start spot extraction pipeline.

rsfish_spark_worker_cores.description: Number of cores allocated to each Spark worker.

rsfish_spark_worker_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory (in GB) allocated to each RS-FISH Spark worker. If not set, defaults to rsfish_spark_worker_cores * rsfish_spark_gb_per_core.

rsfish_spark_executor_cores.description: Number of cores per RS-FISH Spark executor. If not set, defaults to rsfish_spark_worker_cores.

rsfish_spark_executor_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory (in GB) allocated to each RS-FISH Spark executor. If not set, defaults to rsfish_spark_executor_cores * rsfish_spark_gb_per_core.

rsfish_spark_executor_overhead_mem_gb.description: Additional memory overhead (in GB) for each RS-FISH Spark executor.

rsfish_spark_task_cores.description: Number of cores per RS-FISH Spark task. If 0, defaults to 1.

rsfish_spark_driver_cores.description: Number of cores allocated for the Spark driver. Default: 1

rsfish_spark_driver_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory to allocate for the Spark driver. Default: 12g

rsfish_spark_gb_per_core.description: Size of memory (in GB) that is allocated for each core of a Spark worker.
rsfish_spark_gb_per_core.help_text: The total memory usage for extracting spots from one acquisition will be workers * worker_cores * gb_per_core.

rsfish_spark_max_partition_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes to pack into a single partition when reading files. If empty, uses Spark default.

spots_extraction_method.description: Spot extraction method to use: RS_FISH or FISHSPOTS

fishspots_psf_file.description: Path to the PSF file used for fishspots

fishspots_psf_retries.description: Fishspots retries for determining the PSF

fishspots_intensity_th_percentiles.description: Fishspots intensity threshold percentiles defined as 2 float values separated by comma between [0,100].

fishspots_roi.description: Region of interest for fishspots detection defined in voxels as minx,miny,minz,maxx,maxy,maxz.

fishspots_config.description: Fishspots YML config file containing various fishspots parameters

fishspots_dask_workers.description: Number of Dask workers used for fishspots

fishspots_dask_min_workers.description: Minimum number of Dask workers required for fishspots

fishspots_dask_worker_cpus.description: CPUs allocated for a fishspots Dask worker

fishspots_dask_worker_mem_gb.description: Memory allocated for a fishspots Dask worker

fishspots_dask_nthreads.description: Number of threads or tasks per Dask worker for fishspots

fishspots_dask_nprocs.description: Number of processes per Dask worker for fishspots

fishspots_local_workers.description: Number of local processes when a local dask cluster (distributed_spot_extraction=false) is used for fishspots

fishspots_cpus.description: Number of cores allocated for the head fishspots task. Default: 1

fishspots_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory to allocate for the head fishspots task. Default: 12g

fishspots_psf_trim.description: Number of voxels to trim from the PSF edges

fishspots_verbose.description: If true, enable verbose logging for fishspots

spots_features.title: Spots stats and characteristics
spots_features.description: Various spots characteristcs, such as counts, area, intensity

skip_spots_properties.description: If set skip retrieving spot region properties (intensity,...)

spots_counts_subdir.description: data sub-directory used for spots counts

spots_props_subdir.description: data sub-directory used for spots properties

dapi_channel.help_text: The DAPI channel is used as a reference channel for registration, segmentation, and spot extraction.

bleeding_channel.description: Channel (other than DAPI) that needs bleedthrough correction.

spots_counts_process_blocksize.description: Blocksize used to partition a large labels image into smaller blocks that fit in memory. Blocks are processed serially.

spots_counts_cores.description: Number of cores needed for getting spots counts.

spots_counts_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory needed for getting spots counts

spots_timeindex.description: This applies only to OME-ZARR inputs and specify which timeindex to be used for spot extraction

labels_timeindex.description: This applies only to OME-ZARR inputs and specify which labels timeindex to be used for extracting spot region properties

labels_channel.description: This applies only to OME-ZARR inputs and specify which labels channel to be used for extracting spot region properties

spots_props_cores.description: Number of cores needed for getting spots properties (intensity).

spots_props_mem_gb.description: Amount of memory needed for getting spots properties (intensity)

use_inverse_transforms.description: If true, apply inverse transforms when warping spot coordinates.

warp_spots_cpus.description: Number of cpus needed for warping spots.

warp_spots_mem_in_gb.description: Amount of memory needed for warping spots

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

lsf_queue_size.description: Nextflow queue when submitting tasks to LSF

lsf_opts.description: Additional LSF options for job submission

max_job_request_options.title: Max job request options
max_job_request_options.description: Set the top limit for requested resources for any single job.
max_job_request_options.help_text:
If you are running on a smaller system, a pipeline step requesting more resources than are available may cause the Nextflow to stop the run with an error. These options allow you to cap the maximum resources requested by any single job so that the pipeline will run on your system.

Note that you can not _increase_ the resources requested by any job using these options. For that you will need your own configuration file. See [the nf-core website](https://nf-co.re/usage/configuration) for details.

max_cpus.description: Maximum number of CPUs that can be requested for any single job.
max_cpus.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the CPU requirement for each process. Should be an integer e.g. `--max_cpus 1`

max_memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be requested for any single job.
max_memory.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the memory requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. `--max_memory '8.GB'`

max_time.description: Maximum amount of time that can be requested for any single job.
max_time.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the time requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. `--max_time '2.h'`

run_warped_multiscale.description: Override skip logic and force the multiscale warped pyramid to run. Useful when deformations were already run in a previous execution and you want to re-run only the pyramid step.

multiscale_mem_gb.description: Memory resource in GB for multiscale

multiscale_anisotropy.description: If true, preserve anisotropy when generating multiscale pyramids

segmentation_input.description: Segmentation input image - if set this will be the segmentation input

segmentation_subpaths.description: Comma delimited list of segmentation datasets

segmentation_subdir.description: Relative path of the segmentation result

cellpose_input_channels.description: One or more comma separated channels used for segmentation.

cellpose_mask.description: Path to an existing cellpose mask file. If relative, it is resolved against the output directory.

cellpose_mask_subpath.description: Subpath within the cellpose mask file to use as the mask.

run_standalone_merge_labels.description: If true run merge labels as a standalone step (used together with skip_segmentation to merge labels from a previous run)

skip_cellpose_merge_labels.description: If true pass --skip-merge-labels to distributed cellpose, skipping internal label merging

run_segmentation_multiscale.description: If true generate the pyramid for the labels image

sample_channel_axis.description: channel axis for image to be segmented after the timeindex axis was eliminated

sample_z_axis.description: Z axis for image to be segmented after the timeindex axis was eliminated

cellpose_preprocessing_steps.description: Preprocessing steps (There aren't any clear steps yet so ignore for now)

cellpose_preprocessing_config_file.description: YML config file containing preprocessing parameters. However the preprocessing is not clear yet

distributed_cellpose.description: Use a distributed dask cluster to run segmentation

cellpose_dask_config.description: Dask configuration used for distributed cellpose

distributed_cellpose_blocksize.description: Cellpose blocksize used for parallelizing the distributed segmentation

cellpose_blocks_overlap.description: Blocks overlap used for distributed segmentation

segmentation_compression.description: Compression codec used for segmentation output zarr containers

segmentation_compression_opts.description: Compression options (JSON) for segmentation output zarr containers

segmentation_output_chunksize.description: Output blocksize (as CX,CY,CZ) for segmentation results. If not specified it defaults to 128x128x128

cellpose_device.description: GPU device used for cellpose segmentation

cellpose_do_3D.description: Do cellppose segmentation in 3D. For 3D images this must be true

cellpose_no_resampling.description: This is the cellpose no_resample command line argument which if set disables flows resampling to original image size before computing masks.

cellpose_diameter.description: Default cellpose cell size in X,Y,Z. It can specify the dimension for on each axis or if it's a single value the value is the same

cellpose_min_size.description: Minimum number of pixels per mask, can turn off with -1

cellpose_norm_percentile.description: Cellpose normalization args the define the low and high percentile

cellpose_stitch_threshold.description: Cellpose stitch threshold value - compute masks in 2D then stitch them with IOU > 0.9 across planes.

cellprob_threshold.description: Probabilty threshold to control cellpose mask size.

cellpose_labels_distance_th.description: Across labels distance transform threshold for merging labels from adjacent blocks

cellpose_segmentation_cpus.description: Number of CPUs allocated for the segmentation driver

cellpose_segmentation_mem_gb.description: Max memory allocated for the segmentation driver

cellpose_mergelabels_cpus.description: Number of CPUs allocated for the merge labels driver

cellpose_mergelabels_mem_gb.description: Max memory allocated for the merge labels driver

cellpose_dask_min_workers.description: Minimum number of Dask workers required for segmentation

cellpose_dask_worker_cpus.description: CPUs allocated for a cellpose Dask worker

cellpose_dask_worker_mem_gb.description: Memory allocated for a cellpose Dask worker

cellpose_dask_worker_threads.description: Number of concurrent tasks running on a cellpose Dask worker

cellpose_scheduler_port.description: Default scheduler's port of the Dask segmentation cluster

cellpose_dashboard_port.description: Default dashboards port of the Dask segmentation cluster

cellpose_driver_runtime_opts.description: Container runtime options for cellpose driver. When running the segmentation locally (non-distributed) enable GPU access for the main cellpose (--nv)

cellpose_driver_cluster_opts.description: Cluster options for cellpose driver. When running the segmentation locally (non-distributed) we need the main cellpose to be dispatched to a GPU node.

cellpose_worker_runtime_opts.description: Container runtime options for cellpose dask workers. This is used to enable GPU access from the container running cellpose dask workers.

cellpose_worker_cluster_opts.description: Cluster options for cellpose dask workers. This is to allocate cellpose dask workers to nodes that have GPUs, e.g. '-q gpu_l4 -gpu num=1'

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

validationShowHiddenParams.help_text: By default, parameters set as _hidden_ in the schema are not shown on the command line when a user runs with `--help`. Specifying this option will tell the pipeline to show all parameters.

validationFailUnrecognisedParams.description: Validation of parameters fails when an unrecognised parameter is found.
validationFailUnrecognisedParams.help_text: By default, when an unrecognised parameter is found, it returns a warinig.

validationLenientMode.description: Validation of parameters in lenient more.
validationLenientMode.help_text: Allows string values that are parseable as numbers or booleans. For further information see [JSONSchema docs](https://github.com/everit-org/json-schema#lenient-mode).
